metadata.summary: Three-level data modeling (conceptual, logical, physical) across paradigms with normalization and advanced patterns.

content.title: Data Modeling
content.summary: Data modeling at three levels: conceptual (entities and relationships in business terms), logical (normalized attributes, keys, cardinality — technology-agnostic), and physical (tables, data types, indexes for a specific engine). Always progress conceptual → logical → physical; skipping logical leads to unmaintainable schemas.

why.p: Access patterns and data structure determine the correct database paradigm. Poor physical modeling — wrong data types, missing indexes, no partitioning — is the most common source of production performance failures. Database migrations cost 3-6 months and incur 20-30% code rewrites.

list.item: Designing any new persistent data layer (greenfield or migration).
Choosing between database paradigms (relational, document, wide-column, graph, time-series).
Normalizing an existing schema with update anomalies or data duplication.
Adding index strategy or partitioning plan before a table grows beyond 10M rows.

rule.statement: Conceptual: entity names only, high-level relationships, stakeholder communication. Logical: attributes, primary/foreign keys, cardinality, normalization to 3NF. Physical: table/column names, exact data types, indexes, partitioning, engine-specific constraints.

Relational for structured data, complex relationships, ACID required. Document for flexible schema, hierarchical data. Key-Value for extreme performance, simple lookups. Wide-Column for high write throughput, time-series. Graph for highly connected data. Time-Series for timestamped data points, aggregation.
1NF: atomic column values, no repeating groups. 2NF: 1NF + no partial dependencies. 3NF: 2NF + no transitive dependencies. BCNF: every determinant is a candidate key (rarely required).
rule.rationale: 3NF eliminates data duplication and update anomalies. Over-normalization beyond 3NF creates excessive joins without practical benefits.

template.purpose: PostgreSQL standard table with UUID PK, audit columns, soft delete.
MongoDB document schema template with embedded vs referenced patterns.
Cassandra table template with partition key, clustering columns.
